name: outsystems-pipeline

metadata.name: outsystems-pipeline
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: Python package to accelerate the integration of OutSystems with third-party CI/CD tools

documentation:
The outsystems-pipeline Python package provides functions to support the creation of OutSystems CI/CD pipelines using your DevOps automation tool of choice.

Visit the [project repository](https://github.com/OutSystems/outsystems-pipeline) on GitHub for instructions on how to build example OutSystems CI/CD pipelines with common DevOps automation tools, as well as documentation that will help you adapt the examples to your particular scenarios.

# What's new

**Parallel Deployments**

> The following scripts have been updated to enable creating and running parallel deployment plans:
>
> - `deploy_latest_tags_to_target_env.py`
> - `deploy_package_to_target_env.py`
> - `deploy_tags_to_target_env_with_manifest.py`
>
> To enable this feature, use the following parameter:
>
> - `--allow_parallel_deployments`: Skips LifeTime validation for active deployment plans.

**Enhanced Pipeline Operations**

> New pipeline scripts have been added to streamline operations related to manifest files:
>
> - `generate_manifest_file.py`: Generates a trigger manifest file.
> - `validate_manifest_apps_exist_in_target_env.py`: Verifies that manifest applications exist in the target environment.

**Updated Package Dependencies**

> - Updated `requests` dependency to version 2.32.2
> - Added `packaging` dependency, version 24.1

# Installing and upgrading

Install or upgrade outsystems-pipeline to the latest available version as follows:

```
pip install -U outsystems-pipeline
```

modules.name: outsystems.lifetime.lifetime_deployments
documentation: Python Modules

functions.name: outsystems.lifetime.lifetime_deployments.get_deployments
documentation: Returns a list of deployments ordered by creation date, from newest to oldest.

functions.name: outsystems.lifetime.lifetime_deployments.get_deployment_info
documentation: Returns the details of a given deployment, validating if there are any conflicts.
The returned information contains the applications included in the deployment plan and
the possible conflicts that can arise from the deployment of the selected applications.

functions.name: outsystems.lifetime.lifetime_deployments.get_deployment_status
documentation: Returns the details of a given deployment execution, including the deployment status and messages.

functions.name: outsystems.lifetime.lifetime_deployments.get_running_deployment
documentation: Returns the details of the running deployment plan to a specific target environment or empty if nothing is running

functions.name: outsystems.lifetime.lifetime_deployments.get_saved_deployment
documentation: Returns the details of the saved deployment plan to a specific target environment or None if nothing is found

functions.name: outsystems.lifetime.lifetime_deployments.send_deployment
documentation: Creates a deployment to a target environment.
An optional list of applications to include in the deployment can be specified.
The input is a subset of deployment object.

functions.name: outsystems.lifetime.lifetime_deployments.send_binary_deployment
documentation: Creates a deployment to a target environment.
The input is a binary file.

functions.name: outsystems.lifetime.lifetime_deployments.delete_deployment
documentation: Discards a deployment, if possible. Only deployments whose state is “saved” can be deleted.

functions.name: outsystems.lifetime.lifetime_deployments.start_deployment
documentation: Executes the start command in a specified deployment.
The initiation of a deployment plan will check if it's valid.
The applications to redeploy, if applicable, will also be included in the deployment plan.
If continue with errors is allowed, an additional parameter is added to the query string.

functions.name: outsystems.lifetime.lifetime_deployments.continue_deployment
documentation: Executes the continue command in a specified deployment.

functions.name: outsystems.lifetime.lifetime_deployments.check_deployment_two_step_deploy_status
documentation: Function to check if the deployment is with Prepared status
